name: Frijoles De La Olla (Pinto Beans)
recipeInstructions:
1. The day before, wash the beans well and place them in a 3-quart saucepan.
2. Cover with water and let stand overnight.
3. The next day, drain the beans and rinse the saucepan. Return the beans to the saucepan.
4. Cover generously with fresh water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover loosely.
5. Simmer until almost tender, about 1 hour. Add more water if needed to keep the beans covered.
6. Add salt and continue cooking until the beans are tender, about 15 minutes.
7. Mash some of the beans. Boil for an additional 10-15 minutes until the liquid reduces to a thick sauce.
8. Meanwhile, wrap tortillas in foil and place them in a warm oven until heated through, about 30 minutes.
9. Serve the beans in individual bowls, accompanied by warmed tortillas and salsa.
Makes about 3 cups, approximately 6 servings. Each serving, without tortillas or salsa, contains about: 39 calories; 590 mg sodium; 0 cholesterol; 0 fat; 7 grams carbohydrates; 2 grams protein; .86 gram fiber.
